UHL Editorial garden lab Urban Harvest Lab Science-based guides for modern small-space growing.
Home Blog Categories About Contact
Category

Urban Gardening

Urban gardening works best when layout, light, airflow, and container volume are treated as one system. This section focuses on repeatable methods for balconies, rooftops, and tight outdoor spaces.

Primary topic hub

Urban Gardening

Urban gardening is not one technique. It is a set of design decisions around light, root volume, watering, and crop choice inside constrained spaces. This hub organizes the topic into beginner guidance, setup decisions, troubleshooting, and more advanced optimization.

Open topic hub Browse archive

Start with the strongest entry points.

Beginner guides

Best Vegetables for Small Balconies: What Produces Well in Tight Spaces

Topic pathway

Setup guides

These pieces help you choose containers, growing media, and crop mixes that are sustainable to maintain.

Containers February 4, 2026

Container Depth for Tomatoes, Peppers, and Herbs: How Much Root Space Matters

Container size is one of the highest-leverage decisions in small-space gardening. When root volume is too small, every other care task becomes harder.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Soil & Fertilizers February 26, 2026

Potting Mix vs. Raised Bed Soil for Containers: What Actually Belongs in Pots

Container plants need air space, drainage, and predictable structure. That is why raised bed soil and in-ground blends usually disappoint in pots.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Topic pathway

Troubleshooting

Use these guides when balcony herbs or compact crops start drifting off course.

Plant Problems March 5, 2026

Why Are Basil Leaves Turning Yellow Indoors?

Yellow basil rarely has a single universal cause. The pattern of yellowing, the growing medium, and the recent care routine usually tell the story.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Grow Lights February 22, 2026

How Many Hours Should Grow Lights Run for Herbs?

A longer light schedule is not always a better one. Herbs need enough daily light, but they also benefit from a stable dark period and realistic fixture placement.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Topic pathway

Advanced topics

Once the basics are stable, these guides help you extend production or improve indoor support systems.

Grow Lights March 7, 2026

Best Grow Lights for Herbs: What Actually Matters Before You Buy

The best grow light for herbs is the one that matches your footprint, crop density, and tolerance for heat and visual clutter. This guide focuses on fit before hype.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Hydroponics February 18, 2026

Countertop Hydroponic Herbs for Beginners: What to Buy and What to Ignore

Countertop hydroponic systems can work very well for herbs, but only if you treat them as small growing systems rather than decorative appliances.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
